Violin / box / bar plots for gene expression and numeric metadata

PlotFeature.Rd

Plots one panel per feature - either a gene expression value pulled from the specified assay layer or a numeric metadata column - grouped by a categorical metadata variable on the x-axis. Multiple features are assembled with patchwork.

Usage

PlotFeature(
  data,
  features,
  group.by,
  split.by = NULL,
  row.by = NULL,
  exclude = NULL,
  plot_type = c("violin", "box", "both", "bar"),
  add_points = FALSE,
  point_size = 0.3,
  alpha = 0.7,
  add_stats = FALSE,
  comparisons = NULL,
  ref_group = NULL,
  hide_ns = FALSE,
  stat_alpha = 0.05,
  label_format = c("stars", "p.format"),
  ncol = NULL,
  downsample = NULL,
  colors = NULL,
  assay = "RNA",
  layer = "data",
  output_dir = NULL,
  object_name = "",
  pdf_width = NULL,
  pdf_height = NULL
)

Arguments

data

A Seurat object or a plain data frame.

features

Character vector of gene names and/or numeric metadata column names. Mixed vectors are supported.

group.by

Character. Categorical metadata column for the x-axis (groups / conditions).

split.by

Character or NULL. Creates column facets within each feature panel - one panel per level of split.by.

row.by

Character or NULL. Creates row facets within each feature panel. Combined with split.by produces a two-way facet_grid.

exclude

Named list of values to exclude before plotting. Each name is a column name and each value is a character vector of levels to drop. Can target group.by, split.by, row.by, or any other metadata column. Example: list(Cognitive.Status = "Reference", Sex = "Unknown").

plot_type

One of "violin" (default), "box", "both" (violin with a thin white box overlay), or "bar" (mean ± SE bar chart).

add_points

Logical. Overlay jittered individual points. Default FALSE.

point_size

Numeric. Size of jittered points. Default 0.3.

alpha

Numeric. Fill transparency for violins / bars. Default 0.7.

add_stats

Logical. Add Wilcoxon significance brackets via ggsignif. Default FALSE.

comparisons

List of length-2 character vectors specifying pairs to compare. NULL (default) tests all pairwise combinations, or all vs ref_group when that is set.

ref_group

Character or NULL. Reference group for comparisons (all others tested against it).

hide_ns

Logical. Suppress "ns" brackets. Default FALSE.

stat_alpha

Numeric. Significance threshold used for bracket labeling and for determining star levels (* at stat_alpha, ** at stat_alpha / 5, *** at stat_alpha / 50). Default 0.05.

label_format

One of "stars" (default: *, **, ***) or "p.format" (numeric p-value shown).

ncol

Integer or NULL. Columns in the patchwork assembly. NULL = min(n_features, 3).

downsample

Integer or NULL. If supplied, downsample to at most this many cells total (balanced across group.by levels) before plotting. NULL (default) uses all cells.

colors

Named character vector of fill colors for group.by levels. NULL auto-resolves from PrepObject (if a Seurat object is passed) or SelectColors().

assay

Character. Seurat assay to pull gene expression from. Default "RNA".

layer

Character. Assay layer / slot. Default "data" (log-normalized counts).

output_dir

Character or NULL. Save directory. Walks up from PrepObject when a Seurat object is passed.

object_name

Character. Filename prefix. Walks up from PrepObject when a Seurat object is passed.

pdf_width

Numeric or NULL. Override the auto-calculated PDF width in inches. NULL (default) sizes automatically from the number of groups, splits, and features.

pdf_height

Numeric or NULL. Override the auto-calculated PDF height in inches. NULL (default) sizes automatically from the number of row facets and features.

Value

When one feature is requested, a single ggplot2 object. When multiple features are requested, a patchwork combined plot. The plot is also saved as a PDF when output_dir is available.

Details

Feature type is auto-detected: gene names found in the assay are extracted as log-normalized expression; numeric metadata columns are used directly. Passing a categorical metadata column raises a helpful error pointing to the appropriate scSidekick visualization function instead.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Gene expression violin plot
PlotFeature(SeuratObj, features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ"),
  group.by = "CellType")

# Numeric metadata with split + stats, excluding a reference group
PlotFeature(SeuratObj, features = "Age.at.Death",
  group.by  = "Dementia.AD",
  split.by  = "Sex",
  exclude   = list(Cognitive.Status = "Reference"),
  add_stats = TRUE,
  plot_type = "box")

# Two-way facet: split.by columns × row.by rows
PlotFeature(SeuratObj, features = c("CD3E", "LYZ"),
  group.by = "Group",
  split.by = "Timepoint",
  row.by   = "Sex")
} # }
